title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels · Runtime Lens: Letter Frequency Policy
summary: A report labels len(text) as characters, counts newline and combining marks as letters, merges case categories without policy, and presents incomparable metrics under one total. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the letter frequency policy increment in honestly labeled text-metrics panel. Learners progress thr…
objectives: State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-character-grapheme-metrics-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-character-grapheme-metrics-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_character_grapheme_metrics_2(code_points: int = 14, graphemes: int = 11, letters: int = 9, labels_explicit: bool = True, segmentation_transfer_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(code_points, graphemes, letters) < 0:
        return False, code_points, "metric-count"
    if graphemes > code_points or letters > code_points:
        return False, graphemes, "unit-reconciliation"
    if not labels_explicit:
        return False, letters, "ambiguous-unit"
    if not segmentation_transfer_named:
        return False, graphemes, "grapheme-overclaim"
    assert letters <= code_points
    return True, graphemes, "honest-text-metrics"

# Evidence: bookbot-letter-frequency-policy-e0glxf
# Competency: bookbot-letter-frequency-policy.
# Case 9973: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_letter_frequency_policy_e0glxf(code_points: int = 14, graphemes: int = 11, letters: int = 9, labels_explicit: bool = True, segmentation_transfer_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_e0glxf = "e0glxf-fxlg0e-9973"
    assert evidence_variant_e0glxf
    evidence_axes_e0glxf = (
        "corpuse0glxf",
        "policyfxlg0e",
        "revision9973",
        "commande0glxf9973",
        "reportfxlg0e9973",
        "test9973e0glxf",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_e0glxf)) == 6
    if min(code_points, graphemes, letters) < 0:
        return False, code_points, "metric-count"
    if graphemes > code_points or letters > code_points:
        return False, graphemes, "unit-reconciliation"
    if not labels_explicit:
        return False, letters, "ambiguous-unit"
    if not segmentation_transfer_named:
        return False, graphemes, "grapheme-overclaim"
    assert letters <= code_points
    return True, graphemes, "honest-text-metrics"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Letter Frequency Policy model · Theory Letter Frequency Policy, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Letter Frequency Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Letter Frequency Policy model · Theory Letter Frequency Policy, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 4137 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Letter Frequency Policy model · Theory Letter Frequency Policy, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Character frequency has one obvious definition independent of normalization.
